data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Albanese at SubProjects/Albanese
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- SubProjects/Albanese/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0006/sessions/0011-horizon-T6

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next

- Handoff written to `recommendation.md`: attempt `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` first (its DVR input is ready); check v4.31 for valuative-criterion / 0AVF API before hand-rolling; do not re-derive 00OF.

# Recommended focus

Targets:

Prove what is reachable; the 00OF route is a large build, so time-box and report if it does not converge.

# Roadmap slice
- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×1).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- I-0050 info (by=ground, labels=agent-ready, projects=Albanese): [bp-tidy T6] CodimOne history/status purge Prose-purity + status-correctness pass on SubProjects/Albanese/blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex (per inbox I-0049). No math statements changed. - Purged all rendered project-history tokens: commit hashes (b80f227), iter-NNN references, 'axiom-clean since iter-', 'removed as dead code', and bare Lean filenames (StandardSmoothDimension.lean) used as narrative; also dropped several stale % comment blocks. - Corrected now-false status: rewrote passages claiming Stacks 00OF is missing / that the general-point 00OE case 'remains open, gated on 00OF' / that the regular-stalk route is blocked. They now state regularity holds at every p…

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- Recipes: closing GeometricallyReduced / GeometricallyIrreducible scaffold sorries (v4.31 Mathlib has the full API)

Mathlib v4.31 ships `AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/{Basic,Reduced,Irreducible}.lean` — these scaffold instances are now provable, not gaps. Proven patterns (see `Points.lean` `gm_geometricallyReduced`, `ChartIso.lean` `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`, both axiom-clean):

- **Affine case** (`Spec A ⟶ Spec k̄`): `rw [geometricallyReduced_iff / geometricallyIrreducible_iff]`, intro the `IsPullback`, `obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y; algebraize [φ.hom]`, then `h.isoPullback ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso k̄ A K` reduces to `IsReduced/IsDomain (A ⊗[k̄] K)`. Needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (the `(Gm k̄).hom`-to-`Spec.map` defeq).
- **Ring step for localizations**: `IsReduced (Localization.Away t ⊗[k̄] K)` via `Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange k̄ P P _ K` (`≅ Loc ⊗[P] (P ⊗[k̄] K)`), instance `IsLocalization.tensor`, and `isReduced_localizationPreserves` (apply with `by exact` + explicit S — beta-reduction quirk). `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm` give `P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial σ K`.
- **Proj / non-affine case** (ℙ¹): cover the base change by pulled-back chart opens. Per chart: `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero_assoc` + `← Spec.map_comp` + `change` collapses `awayι ≫ hom` to `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`, then the affine recipe. Covering: `Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top` with the (now named) `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span`. Intersection: any preimage of the generic point `⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, …⟩` under `pullback.fst` (surjective — `Surjective` is `IsStableUnderBaseChange`, `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst`). Assemble with the new `irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens` (ChartIso).
- **Traps**: inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, ring-level `IsReduced` must be `_root_.IsReduced`; `⊗[k̄]` notation needs `open scoped TensorProduct in` BEFORE the docstring; avoid `ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _` (leaves an unsynthesizable eta-expanded `IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms` goal — use `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (iso).hom` or `.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff` instead); the affine cover's `I₀`/`𝒰.f i` are semireducibly opaque — work with `Proj.awayι (X i)` + `fin_cases` normalization (`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux` idiom) rather than cover indices.
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does).

**SUPERSEDED for the all-points case (run 0006 T6): Stacks 00OF is NO LONGER needed.** The old claim here — "remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` is Stacks 00OF, do not attempt" — is now false. See [[I-0047]]: `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` proves regularity at EVERY prime of a standard-smooth algebra over a perfect field, Serre-free (trdeg–height induction via Stacks 00ON + conormal split-exactness from `FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`), so `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` / `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` are now axiom-clean. This closed-point recipe is retained only for its k̄-rationality trap and the maximal-ideal API details.
- Recipe: smooth-over-k̄ ⇒ reduced is CLOSED; Stacks 00NP (regular local ⇒ domain) EXISTS in-project — do not re-derive either.

- **`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`** (`AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, public, axiom-clean): any Noetherian regular local ring is a domain. This is the full Stacks `00NP`; docstrings elsewhere calling it a "Mathlib gap" predate its closure. Companions: `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (R/(x) regular of spanFinrank −1 for `x ∈ 𝔪∖𝔪²`), `matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent` (§3.C, cotangent-independent ⇒ regular sequence).
- **Smooth over `Spec k̄` ⇒ reduced**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` (`CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B Step B.f) + ring-level `isReduced_of_isStandardSmooth_of_isAlgClosed` (Step B.e). Proof pattern that works at *every* point (no 00OF needed): reduced is stalk-local (`isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`) + detected at maximal localisations (`isReduced_ofLocalizationMaximal`), and B.d′ closed-point regularity + 00NP give those. Contrast: stalk *regularity* at non-closed points stays 00OF-blocked.
- Useful glue found in v4.31 Mathlib: `RingHom.isStandardSmooth_respectsIso` (`.2` = precompose ring equiv — transports a standard-smooth presentation across `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv` base identification), `RingHom.isStandardSmooth_algebraMap`, `isReduced_localizationPreserves`, `isReduced_of_noZeroDivisors`.
- Mathlib does NOT have: smooth ⇒ reduced/geometrically-reduced at scheme level (checked `Geometrically/Reduced.lean`, `Morphisms/Smooth.lean`, `RingTheory/Smooth/*`), nor regular-local ⇒ domain (`RegularLocalRing/` has only `Defs.lean`). `Group/Smooth.lean` goes the other way (geom. reduced group scheme ⇒ smooth).
- Iteration trick: a root-level scratch `import Mathlib + import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CodimOneExtension` type-checks in ~4 min against warm oleans; `open private <name> from <module>` lets `#print axioms` reach private decls.
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).
- **Prove 'pullback of pushforward is a pushforward' as essImage membership, with the ring square the ABSTRACT pushout** `B := pushout φ ψ` (`IsPushout.of_hasPushout`): geometric cartesianness is free (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), `Spec σ` open-immersion by stability, its range via `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd` + `hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd` + `Set.range_eq_univ.mpr h.surjective`, and the final match with the preimage open by `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pushforwardComp/Congr`. NO four-corner isoSpec transport of a concrete square.
- **`pullback e.hom ≅ pushforward e.inv`** (scheme iso e): `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom) (pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction` — one-liner, no defeq wall.
- **Open-open pushforward–restriction commutation**: the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` 4-step pattern (`SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso(eqToIso) ≪≫ pushforwardCongr ≪≫ pushforwardComp.symm`) transfers verbatim to the square `opensInterLift V₀ U ≫ V₀.ι = (U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι ≫ U.ι`; the appLE-coherence proof is the same 5 lines.
- **Pointwise scheme-morphism reasoning**: derive morphism-level equalities first (cancel isos with `cancel_mono`), then apply at points with `Scheme.Hom.comp_apply`; raw `ConcreteCategory.hom`-coercion mismatches from `Set.mem_image` unfold are defeq — bridge with `have h' : U.ι y = V₀.ι x := hyx` + `show`.
- **Names**: `Opens.grothendieckTopology` (root ns), `Scheme.Hom.comp_apply`, `Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage`, `IsAffineOpen.inf` (needs `[IsAffineHom (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X))]` ⟸ `IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal`), `Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion`, `IsOpenImmersion.of_comp`.
- The whole 430-LOC file (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`) compiled with ~3 error-fix iterations — the iso-chain compositions (`pushforwardComp/Congr/Id .app` chains) hit NO v4.31 transparency walls.
- Pic0Scheme LFT tidy gate NEGATIVE — HasPicScheme packages no finiteness data

- Checked in T5 (run 0005): `LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom` **cannot resolve by synthesis** — `HasPicScheme` (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`) packages only the `RepresentableBy` existential, and no LFT instance for `PicScheme` exists anywhere in the cone.
- The marginal tidy `Pic0Scheme := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` therefore needs a NEW `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` typed-sorry instance class first (mathematically sound: Kleiman §4, Pic is LFT as a disjoint union of open quasi-projectives). Net sorry-token count unchanged, so it was deferred.
- `SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent` (green, byte-identical to AJC) is the place to attempt it; 3 isolated DAG nodes there (`IsCechAcyclicCover` trio) are pruning artifacts, not blueprint bugs.
- **Horizon session write scope can be broader than the task's `write_set` — check the session globs before declaring blueprint work out of reach.**

- In run 0006 T6, the task file listed a Lean-only `write_set`, and Ground concluded (I-0038/I-0039) that only Ground could do the Lean+blueprint dead-code cleanup. The actual Horizon session ran with scope glob `SubProjects/Albanese/**`, which includes `blueprint/` — so Horizon completed the one-pass cleanup itself.
- Rule of thumb: the task `files=` list is a *suggestion*; the binding constraint is the session's write-scope globs. Verify against those before rerouting work to Ground.
- **Stacks 04KV/037Q descent CLOSED** — `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` is axiom-clean (T5, run 0005 session 0007).

- New module `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean` (~330 LOC, Mathlib-only imports, sorry-free, axioms = propext/choice/Quot.sound): Stacks 05P3 (`A ⊗[k] B` domain over alg-closed `k`, via Nullstellensatz-evaluation + Jacobson-radical coordinate argument + f.g.-subalgebra exhaustion), then geometry: fibers of `X_L → X` are `Spec(K ⊗ L)`; alg-closed base via Mathlib's `GeometricallyConnected.connectedSpace`; algebraic-closure step via open+closed(integral)+singleton-fiber clopen argument; assembly `k ⊆ k̄ ↪ L̄ ⊇ L`.
- Key Mathlib enablers already present in v4.31: `UniversallyOpen` instance for morphisms to Spec-of-field (Stacks 0383), `IsIntegralHom → UniversallyClosed`, the `geometrically` framework, `Scheme.Hom.fiber` API, `descResidueField_stalkClosedPointTo_fromSpecResidueField` for the section retraction.
- `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` is now axiom-clean automatically. IdentityComponent sorries: 9 → 8.
- **Unblocked next**: `baseChangeIso` iso slot (clopen-image identification, ~80-100 LOC per in-file plan) and `isSubgroupHomomorphism` (`G⁰ ×ₖ G⁰` connected now derivable via the Mathlib pullback instance). Remaining Pic⁰-specific sorries still FGA-blocked.
- **Stale mirror**: `SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent` is now BEHIND AJC (was byte-identical); re-sync needs `IdentityComponent.lean` + the new `GeometricallyConnectedSection.lean` + blueprint chapter.
- **Recipe: Stacks 00TT at ARBITRARY primes over a perfect field, Serre-free (supersedes the "00OF is the remaining genuine gap / do not attempt" guidance — 00OF is no longer needed).**

Proven in `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` (axiom-clean, v4.31 Mathlib suffices). Structure, reusable for similar dimension-theory builds:

- **Lemma A (trdeg–height)**: for prime `P ⊆ k[x_ι]`, `∃ d ≤ trdeg k (k[x_ι]⧸P)` with `#ι ≤ ht P + d`. Induction via `Finite.induction_empty_option`; one-variable step uses Stacks 00ON `Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown` (+ `Algebra.HasGoingDown.of_flat`, `Module.Flat.of_free`) and splits on the fiber prime in `(R⧸p)[X]` (`Ideal.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial`); the `∃ d ≤`-witness form avoids ALL fraction fields and exact-trdeg bookkeeping. Key trdeg API: `trdeg_add_le`, `trdeg_le_of_injective`, `Polynomial.trdeg_of_isDomain` (all root-namespace!).
- **Lemma C**: `rank Ω[K⁄k] = trdeg k K` for `EssFiniteType` field ext of perfect `k`: `exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField` (returns a `Finset`!) + `Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable` + `tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale` + `AlgebraicIndependent.aevalEquivField` + `Basis.ofIsLocalizedModule` over `mvPolynomialBasis`.
- **Lemma D**: `dim_κ m/m² + dim_κ Ω[κ⁄R] = n` generalising iter-199 by dropping `Subsingleton Ω[κ/R]`: same `iff_split_injection` retraction + `exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` + `mapBaseChange_surjective` + rank–nullity. `FormallySmooth k κ` is FREE from the new low-priority instance `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField [EssFiniteType k κ]`.
- Assembly: `finrank Cot ≤ ht q` by ℕ∞ cancellation (`ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`, pass `k :=` explicitly), then `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`.

**Traps**: (1) NEVER bridge AlgEquiv/RingEquiv coercion paths with `Iff.rfl`/`rfl` at FUNCTION level (whnf timeout on `renameEquiv`); state helpers with pointwise membership hypotheses (`∀ a, a ∈ P₀ ↔ g a ∈ P`) instead. (2) `have`-bound equivs are opaque — use `set ... with` when a later proof must compute through the definition. (3) In `of_equiv` induction branches don't `rename_i` (grabs instances); avoid naming the types. (4) Multi-step `rw` chains on algebraMap-towers can close the goal mid-list and error — split into per-equation `have`s. (5) `Basis` is now `Module.Basis` (open Module).

Dead code note: `stalkMap_flat_of_smooth` (Stage 1) and `exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth` (Stage 3) in `CodimOneExtension.lean` now have NO Lean consumers (the new stalk proof bypasses them); blueprint-pinned leaves — Ground may prune or keep.
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

1381	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1382	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1383	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1384	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) :
1385	    ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, 2 ≤ Order.coheight z := by
1386	  sorry
1387	
1388	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1389	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1390	
1391	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1392	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1393	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1394	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1395	
1396	The proof combines two steps:
1397	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1398	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1399	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1400	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1401	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1402	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1403	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1404	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1405	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1406	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1407	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1408	
1409	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1410	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1411	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1412	
1413	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1414	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1415	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1416	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1417	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1418	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1419	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1420	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1421	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1422	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1423	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1424	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1425	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1426	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1427	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1428	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1429	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1430	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1431	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1432	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1433	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1434	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1435	  --
1436	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1437	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1438	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1439	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1440	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1441	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1442	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1443	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1444	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1445	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1446	  --
1447	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1448	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1449	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1450	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1451	  --
1452	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1453	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.
1454	  sorry
1455	
1456	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1457	
1458	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1459	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1460	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1461	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1462	
1463	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1464	
1465	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1466	codim 1.**
1467	
1468	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1469	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1470	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1471	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1472	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1473	
1474	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1475	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1476	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1477	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1478	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1479	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1480	
1481	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1482	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1483	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1484	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1485	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1486	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1487	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1488	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1489	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1490	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1491	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1492	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1493	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1494	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1495	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1496	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1497	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1498	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1499	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1500	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1501	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1502	  --
1503	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1504	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1505	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1506	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1507	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1508	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1509	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1510	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1511	  --   the domain of definition.
1512	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1513	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1514	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1515	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1516	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1517	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1518	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1519	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1520	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1521	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1522	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1523	  --   not packaged.
1524	  --
1525	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1526	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1527	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1528	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).
1529	  sorry
1530	
1531	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1532	
1533	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1534	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1535	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1536	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1537	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1538	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1539	witnesses are constructed in this project).

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.WeilDivisor
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CoheightBridge
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AuslanderBuchsbaum
10	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.StandardSmoothDimension
11	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.SmoothPrimeRegularity
12	
13	/-!
14	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
15	
16	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
17	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
18	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
19	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
20	
21	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
22	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
23	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
24	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
25	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
26	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
27	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
28	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
29	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
30	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
31	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
32	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
33	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
34	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
35	  outside this iter's scope.
36	
37	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
38	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
39	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
40	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
41	
42	## Status (run-0006 T6)
43	
44	Of the blueprint-pinned declarations below, the definitional layer (1, 2, 6)
45	and the DVR chain (3, via the now sorry-free
46	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` — Stacks 00TT at every point, proved
47	Serre-free in `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean`) are complete and
48	axiom-clean. The remaining `sorry`s are:
49	`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (the unbundled codim-≥2 half
50	of Milne 3.1, gated on rational-map valuative-criterion plumbing),
51	`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (4; additionally needs the Step-2
52	depth-≥2 / local-cohomology extension, Stacks 0AVF), and
53	`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (5; needs the function-field
54	pullback machinery).
55	
56	The 6 pinned declarations are:
57	
58	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
59	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
60	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
61	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
62	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
63	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
64	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
65	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
66	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
67	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
68	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
69	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
70	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
71	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
72	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
73	   header).
74	
75	## Note on type expressivity
76	
77	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
78	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
79	
80	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
81	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
82	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
83	  not `True`.
84	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
85	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
86	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
87	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
88	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
89	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
90	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
91	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
92	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
93	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
94	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
98	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
99	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
100

data.content:
/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Andrew Yang
-/
module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.SpreadingOut
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated
/-!

# Rational maps between schemes

## Main definitions

* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PartialMap`: A partial map from `X` to `Y` (`X.PartialMap Y`) is
  a morphism into `Y` defined on a dense open subscheme of `X`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PartialMap.equiv`:
  Two partial maps are equivalent if they are equal on a dense open subscheme.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap`:
  A rational map from `X` to `Y` (`X ⤏ Y`) is an equivalence class of partial maps.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.equivFunctionFieldOver`:
  Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
  `S`-morphisms `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` correspond bijectively to `S`-rational maps from `X` to `Y`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`:
  If `X` is reduced and `Y` is separated, then any `f : X ⤏ Y` can be realized as a partial
  map on `f.domain`, the domain of definition of `f`.
-/

@[expose] public section

universe u

open CategoryTheory hiding Quotient

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {X Y Z S : Scheme.{u}} (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S)

namespace Scheme

/--
A partial map from `X` to `Y` (`X.PartialMap Y`) is a morphism into `Y`
defined on a dense open subscheme of `X`.
-/
structure PartialMap (X Y : Scheme.{u}) where
  /-- The domain of definition of a partial map. -/
  domain : X.Opens
  dense_domain : Dense (domain : Set X)
  /-- The underlying morphism of a partial map. -/
  hom : ↑domain ⟶ Y

variable (S) in
/-- A partial map is an `S`-map if the underlying morphism is. -/
abbrev PartialMap.IsOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X.PartialMap Y) :=
  f.hom.IsOver S

namespace PartialMap

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
lemma isOver_iff_eq_restrict [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] {f : X.PartialMap Y} :
    f.IsOver S ↔ f.compHom (Y ↘ S) = (X ↘ S).toPartialMap.restrict _ f.dense_domain (by simp) := by
  simp [PartialMap.ext_iff]

/-- If `x` is in the domain of a partial map `f`, then `f` restricts to a map from `Spec 𝒪_x`. -/
noncomputable
def fromSpecStalkOfMem (f : X.PartialMap Y) {x} (hx : x ∈ f.domain) :
    Spec (X.presheaf.stalk x) ⟶ Y :=
  f.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem x hx ≫ f.hom

/-- A partial map restricts to a map from `Spec K(X)`. -/
noncomputable
abbrev fromFunctionField [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X.PartialMap Y) :
    Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y :=
  f.fromSpecStalkOfMem
    ((genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open f.domain.2 f.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma fromSpecStalkOfMem_restrict (f : X.PartialMap Y)
    {U : X.Opens} (hU : Dense (U : Set X)) (hU' : U ≤ f.domain) {x} (hx : x ∈ U) :
    (f.restrict U hU hU').fromSpecStalkOfMem hx = f.fromSpecStalkOfMem (hU' hx) := by
  dsimp only [fromSpecStalkOfMem, restrict, Scheme.Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem]
  have e : ⟨x, hU' hx⟩ = X.homOfLE hU' ⟨x, hx⟩ := by
    rw [Scheme.homOfLE_base]
    rfl
  rw [Category.assoc, ← SpecMap_stalkMap_fromSpecStalk_assoc,
    ← SpecMap_stalkSpecializes_fromSpecStalk (Inseparable.of_eq e).specializes,
    ← TopCat.Presheaf.stalkCongr_inv _ (Inseparable.of_eq e)]
  simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp]
  congr 3
  rw [Iso.eq_inv_comp, ← Category.assoc, IsIso.comp_inv_eq, IsIso.eq_inv_comp,
    Hom.stalkMap_congr_hom _ _ (X.homOfLE_ι hU').symm]
  simp only [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkCongr_hom]
  rw [← Hom.stalkSpecializes_stalkMap_assoc, Hom.stalkMap_comp]

lemma fromFunctionField_restrict (f : X.PartialMap Y) [IrreducibleSpace X]
    {U : X.Opens} (hU : Dense (U : Set X)) (hU' : U ≤ f.domain) :
    (f.restrict U hU hU').fromFunctionField = f.fromFunctionField :=
  fromSpecStalkOfMem_restrict f _ _ _

/--
Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and
`X` is irreducible germ-injective at `x` (e.g. when `X` is integral),
any `S`-morphism `Spec 𝒪ₓ ⟶ Y` spreads out to a partial map from `X` to `Y`.
-/
noncomputable
def ofFromSpecStalk [IrreducibleSpace X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] {x : X} [X.IsGermInjectiveAt x]
    (φ : Spec (X.presheaf.stalk x) ⟶ Y) (h : φ ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk x ≫ sX) : X.PartialMap Y where
  hom := (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
  domain := (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose
  dense_domain := (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose.2.dense
    ⟨_, (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose_spec.choose⟩

lemma ofFromSpecStalk_comp [IrreducibleSpace X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
    {x : X} [X.IsGermInjectiveAt x] (φ : Spec (X.presheaf.stalk x) ⟶ Y)
    (h : φ ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk x ≫ sX) :
    (ofFromSpecStalk sX sY φ h).hom ≫ sY = (ofFromSpecStalk sX sY φ h).domain.ι ≫ sX :=
  (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2

lemma mem_domain_ofFromSpecStalk [IrreducibleSpace X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
    {x : X} [X.IsGermInjectiveAt x] (φ : Spec (X.presheaf.stalk x) ⟶ Y)
    (h : φ ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk x ≫ sX) : x ∈ (ofFromSpecStalk sX sY φ h).domain :=
  (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose_spec.choose

lemma fromSpecStalkOfMem_ofFromSpecStalk [IrreducibleSpace X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
    {x : X} [X.IsGermInjectiveAt x] (φ : Spec (X.presheaf.stalk x) ⟶ Y)
    (h : φ ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk x ≫ sX) :
    (ofFromSpecStalk sX sY φ h).fromSpecStalkOfMem (mem_domain_ofFromSpecStalk sX sY φ h) = φ :=
  (spread_out_of_isGermInjective' sX sY φ h).choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1.symm

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
@[simp]
lemma fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom (f : X.PartialMap Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (x) (hx) :
    (f.compHom g).fromSpecStalkOfMem (x := x) hx = f.fromSpecStalkOfMem hx ≫ g := by
  simp [fromSpecStalkOfMem]

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
@[simp]
lemma fromSpecStalkOfMem_toPartialMap (f : X ⟶ Y) (x) :
    f.toPartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem (x := x) trivial = X.fromSpecStalk x ≫ f := by
  simp [fromSpecStalkOfMem]

/-- Two partial maps are equivalent if they are equal on a dense open subscheme. -/
protected noncomputable
def equiv (f g : X.PartialMap Y) : Prop :=
  ∃ (W : X.Opens) (hW : Dense (W : Set X)) (hWl : W ≤ f.domain) (hWr : W ≤ g.domain),
    (f.restrict W hW hWl).hom = (g.restrict W hW hWr).hom

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
lemma equivalence_rel : Equivalence (@Scheme.PartialMap.equiv X Y) where
  exact ext_of_isDominant (ι := X.homOfLE hU') (by simpa using H.1)

section functionField

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- A rational map restricts to a map from `Spec K(X)`. -/
noncomputable
def RationalMap.fromFunctionField [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X ⤏ Y) :
    Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y := by
  refine Quotient.lift PartialMap.fromFunctionField ?_ f
  intro f g ⟨W, hW, hWl, hWr, e⟩
  have : f.restrict W hW hWl = g.restrict W hW hWr := by
    ext1
    · rfl
    rw [e]; simp
  rw [← f.fromFunctionField_restrict hW hWl, this, g.fromFunctionField_restrict]

@[simp]
lemma RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X.PartialMap Y) :
    f.toRationalMap.fromFunctionField = f.fromFunctionField := rfl

/--
Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
any `S`-morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` spreads out to a rational map from `X` to `Y`.
-/
noncomputable
def RationalMap.ofFunctionField [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
    (f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y) (h : f ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk _ ≫ sX) : X ⤏ Y :=
  (PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk sX sY f h).toRationalMap

lemma RationalMap.fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY]
    (f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y) (h : f ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk _ ≫ sX) :
    (ofFunctionField sX sY f h).fromFunctionField = f :=
  PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ofFromSpecStalk sX sY _ _

lemma RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq [IsIntegral X] (f g : X.RationalMap Y)
    (H : f.fromFunctionField = g.fromFunctionField) : f = g := by
  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := g.exists_rep
  refine PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ?_
  exact PartialMap.equiv_of_fromSpecStalkOfMem_eq _ _ _ _ H

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/--
Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
`S`-morphisms `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` correspond bijectively to `S`-rational maps from `X` to `Y`.
-/
noncomputable
def RationalMap.equivFunctionField [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] :
    { f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y // f ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk _ ≫ sX } ≃
      { f : X ⤏ Y // f.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap } where
  toFun f := ⟨.ofFunctionField sX sY f f.2, PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr
      ⟨_, PartialMap.dense_domain _, le_rfl, le_top, by simp [PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk_comp]⟩⟩
  invFun f := ⟨f.1.fromFunctionField, by
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := f
    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
    simpa [fromFunctionField_toRationalMap] using! congr(RationalMap.fromFunctionField $hf)⟩
  left_inv f := Subtype.ext (RationalMap.fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _)
  right_inv f := Subtype.ext (RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq
      (ofFunctionField sX sY f.1.fromFunctionField _) f
      (RationalMap.fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _))

/--
Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
`S`-morphisms `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` correspond bijectively to `S`-rational maps from `X` to `Y`.
-/
noncomputable
def RationalMap.equivFunctionFieldOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] [IsIntegral X]
    [LocallyOfFiniteType (Y ↘ S)] :
    { f : Spec X.functionField ⟶ Y // f.IsOver S } ≃ { f : X ⤏ Y // f.IsOver S } :=
  ((Equiv.subtypeEquivProp (by simp only [Hom.isOver_iff]; rfl)).trans
    (RationalMap.equivFunctionField (X ↘ S) (Y ↘ S))).trans
      (Equiv.subtypeEquivProp (by ext f; rw [RationalMap.isOver_iff]))

end functionField

section domain

/-- The domain of definition of a rational map. -/
def RationalMap.domain (f : X ⤏ Y) : X.Opens :=
  sSup { PartialMap.domain g | (g) (_ : g.toRationalMap = f) }

lemma PartialMap.le_domain_toRationalMap (f : X.PartialMap Y) :
    f.domain ≤ f.toRationalMap.domain :=
  le_sSup ⟨f, rfl, rfl⟩

lemma RationalMap.mem_domain {f : X ⤏ Y} {x} :
    x ∈ f.domain ↔ ∃ g : X.PartialMap Y, x ∈ g.domain ∧ g.toRationalMap = f :=
  TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_sSup.trans (by simp [@and_comm (x ∈ _)])

lemma RationalMap.dense_domain (f : X ⤏ Y) : Dense (X := X) f.domain :=
  f.inductionOn (fun g ↦ g.dense_domain.mono g.le_domain_toRationalMap)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The open cover of the domain of `f : X ⤏ Y`,
consisting of all the domains of the partial maps in the equivalence class. -/
noncomputable
def RationalMap.openCoverDomain (f : X ⤏ Y) : f.domain.toScheme.OpenCover where
  I₀ := { PartialMap.domain g | (g) (_ : g.toRationalMap = f) }
  X U := U.1.toScheme
  f U := X.homOfLE (le_sSup U.2)

/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang, Qi Ge, Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Andrew Yang, Qi Ge, Christian Merten
-/
module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Immersion
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.RingHom.Injective
public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Valuation.LocalSubring

/-!
# Valuative criterion

## Main results

- `AlgebraicGeometry.UniversallyClosed.eq_valuativeCriterion`:
  A morphism is universally closed if and only if
  it is quasi-compact and satisfies the existence part of the valuative criterion.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.IsSeparated.eq_valuativeCriterion`:
  A morphism is separated if and only if
  it is quasi-separated and satisfies the uniqueness part of the valuative criterion.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.eq_valuativeCriterion`:
  A morphism is proper if and only if
  it is qcqs and of finite type and satisfies the valuative criterion.

## Future projects
Show that it suffices to check discrete valuation rings when the base is Noetherian.

-/

@[expose] public section

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

/--
A valuative commutative square over a morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is a square
```
Spec K ⟶ Y
  |       |
  ↓       ↓
Spec R ⟶ X
```
where `R` is a valuation ring, and `K` is its ring of fractions.

We are interested in finding lifts `Spec R ⟶ Y` of this diagram.
-/
structure ValuativeCommSq {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) where
  /-- The valuation ring of a valuative commutative square. -/
  R : Type u
  [commRing : CommRing R]
  [domain : IsDomain R]
  [valuationRing : ValuationRing R]
  /-- The field of fractions of a valuative commutative square. -/
  K : Type u
  [field : Field K]
  [algebra : Algebra R K]
  [isFractionRing : IsFractionRing R K]
  /-- The top map in a valuative commutative map. -/
  (i₁ : Spec (.of K) ⟶ X)
  /-- The bottom map in a valuative commutative map. -/
  (i₂ : Spec (.of R) ⟶ Y)
  (commSq : CommSq i₁ (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R K))) f i₂)

namespace ValuativeCommSq

attribute [instance] commRing domain valuationRing field algebra isFractionRing

end ValuativeCommSq

/-- A morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` satisfies the existence part of the valuative criterion if
every valuative commutative square over `f` has (at least) a lift. -/
def ValuativeCriterion.Existence : MorphismProperty Scheme :=
  fun _ _ f ↦ ∀ S : ValuativeCommSq f, S.commSq.HasLift

/-- A morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` satisfies the uniqueness part of the valuative criterion if
every valuative commutative square over `f` has at most one lift. -/
def ValuativeCriterion.Uniqueness : MorphismProperty Scheme :=
  fun _ _ f ↦ ∀ S : ValuativeCommSq f, Subsingleton S.commSq.LiftStruct

/-- A morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` satisfies the valuative criterion if
every valuative commutative square over `f` has a unique lift. -/
def ValuativeCriterion : MorphismProperty Scheme :=
  fun _ _ f ↦ ∀ S : ValuativeCommSq f, Nonempty (Unique (S.commSq.LiftStruct))

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)

lemma ValuativeCriterion.iff {f : X ⟶ Y} :
    ValuativeCriterion f ↔ Existence f ∧ Uniqueness f := by
  change (∀ _, _) ↔ (∀ _, _) ∧ (∀ _, _)
  simp_rw [← forall_and, unique_iff_subsingleton_and_nonempty, and_comm, CommSq.HasLift.iff]

lemma ValuativeCriterion.eq :
    ValuativeCriterion = Existence ⊓ Uniqueness := by
  ext X Y f
  exact iff

lemma ValuativeCriterion.existence {f : X ⟶ Y} (h : ValuativeCriterion f) :
    ValuativeCriterion.Existence f := (iff.mp h).1

lemma ValuativeCriterion.uniqueness {f : X ⟶ Y} (h : ValuativeCriterion f) :
    ValuativeCriterion.Uniqueness f := (iff.mp h).2

namespace ValuativeCriterion.Existence

open IsLocalRing

@[stacks 01KE]
lemma specializingMap (H : ValuativeCriterion.Existence f) :
    SpecializingMap f := by
  intro x' y h
  let stalk_y_to_residue_x' : Y.presheaf.stalk y ⟶ X.residueField x' :=
    Y.presheaf.stalkSpecializes h ≫ f.stalkMap x' ≫ X.residue x'
  obtain ⟨A, hA, hA_local⟩ := exists_factor_valuationRing stalk_y_to_residue_x'.hom
  let stalk_y_to_A : Y.presheaf.stalk y ⟶ .of A :=

80:  Scheme.IsGermInjectiveAt.cond

86:  obtain ⟨U, hx, hU, H⟩ := Scheme.IsGermInjectiveAt.cond (x := x)
90:instance (x : X) [X.IsGermInjectiveAt x] [IsOpenImmersion f] :
91:    Y.IsGermInjectiveAt (f x) := by
102:    Y.IsGermInjectiveAt (f x) ↔ X.IsGermInjectiveAt x := by

126:    {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [∀ i, (𝒰.X i).IsGermInjective] : X.IsGermInjective := by

input.description: Read Thm32 consumer of the codimGe2 lemma

data.content:
Mathlib gap is discharged by the project-side
`isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`
(§3.B Step B.e): stalks are localisations of standard-smooth `k̄`-algebras,
whose maximal-ideal localisations are regular local (Step B.d′) hence domains
(`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`, Stacks `00NP` in
`AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`), and reducedness is checked at maximal
localisations then transported to stalks and glued. The hypothesis is
accordingly strengthened from `[Field kbar]` to `[Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed
kbar]` (its unique consumer `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` already
assumes algebraic closure). Axiom-clean. -/
private theorem isReduced_of_smooth_over_field
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth A.hom] :
    IsReduced A.left :=
  isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed A

/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**

For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.

**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:

1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.

The implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left` — formerly the substantive
Mathlib gap of this helper — is now supplied project-side by
`isReduced_of_smooth_over_field` (backed by
`isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`),
so this helper is **axiom-clean** as of run 0002, T3 session 0021.

This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
rational map `f`. -/
private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
    IsIntegral A.left := by
  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
  -- Step 3: smoothness over the algebraically closed base field forces
  -- reducedness of the source (`isReduced_of_smooth_over_field`, closed in
  -- run 0002 session 0021 via `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed`).
  haveI : IsReduced A.left := isReduced_of_smooth_over_field A
  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left

/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
Milne's two-input argument.**

For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).

**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:

1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
   either empty or pure codim 1.
2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.

Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.

**Status (run-0006 T6).** The codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 is now
exposed as the standalone lemma
`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` in
`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (itself a named `sorry`, gated on the
rational-map valuative-criterion machinery), and this helper's body is
`sorry`-free: branch 2 derives the contradiction `2 ≤ coheight x = 1`
directly from that lemma.

This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
substantive missing content localised here. -/
private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    CodimOneFree f := by
  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
  intro x hx
  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
    by_contra hnotin
    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
    rw [hempty] at hxZ
    exact hxZ.elim
  · -- **Branch 2 (CLOSED, run-0006 T6).** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
    -- Milne 3.1 (`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`, the
    -- unbundled Step-1 half of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`, now
    -- exposed as a standalone lemma in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`)
    -- contradicts `coheight x = 1` for a codim-1 point of the indeterminacy
    -- locus, so `x ∈ f.domain`. (The pure-codim-1 disjunct of Milne 3.3 is
    -- not needed once the codim-≥2 conclusion is available.)
    by_contra hnotin
    have h2 := indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete f x hnotin
    rw [hx] at h2
    norm_num at h2

/-- **Iter-182 documentation helper: branch-1 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`.**
The empty-indeterminacy branch of Milne Lemma 3.3 closes `CodimOneFree`
without depending on the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1. Exposed as a
standalone lemma so the structural split survives any future refactor of
the parent helper. Axiom-clean. -/
private theorem codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X.RationalMap Y}
    (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) : CodimOneFree f := by
  intro x _
  by_contra hnotin
  have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
  rw [h] at hxZ
  exact hxZ.elim

/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
for `extend_to_av`.**

The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
its former `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap now closed axiom-clean) and
`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).

This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,

input.description: Read remainder of Thm32 file

data.content:
⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩

/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**

For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).

This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
`J → A`.

**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
Milne's two-line argument:

1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
   by the caller.

2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
   half of Milne 3.1 (`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`,
   the named-`sorry` lemma in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` that now
   carries the remaining math content).

3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).

The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
theorem extend_to_av
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
  haveI := hint
  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod

end RationalMap

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

467:(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
510:`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (A.4.c) and Milne Corollary I.1.2
518:`extend_to_av` rational-map extension, and the connecting biconditional
`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).

The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.

iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed
`sorry`. -/
theorem descentThroughBirationalSigma
    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
      symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ := by
  sorry

    have hmapeq : (List.ofFn (fun i : Fin m => rs i.succ)).map
        (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
